Noxious Weeds Act CAP. 19.05Section 1
C
T
NOXIOUS WEEDS ACT
AN ACT TO PROVIDE FOR THE ERADICATION OF PLANTS
HARMFUL TO AGRICULTURE1
Commencement [4th September, 1917]
1 Short title
This Act may be cited as the Noxious Weeds Act.
2 Interpretation
In this Act —
“Minister” means the Minister responsible for forests;2
“noxious weed” means any plant proclaimed by the Minister with the consent
of the Cabinet to be a noxious weed.3
3 Power to proclaim noxious weeds
The Minister with the consent of the Cabinet may from time to time proclaim any
plant to be a noxious weed within the whole or any part of Tonga.4
4 Penalty
Any owner or occupier who fails to use every means to eradicate a noxious weed
from his holding shall be guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding
$250.5
to 2020 Revised Edition Page 3

Section 5 CAP. 19.05 Noxious Weeds Act
5 Duty of inspectors
Every inspector under the Plant Quarantine Act shall report the presence on any land
of any noxious weed to the police who shall prosecute the owner or occupier. In any
district where no inspector under the Plant Quarantine Act has been appointed the
police shall prosecute offenders against this Act.6
Page 4 2020 Revised Edition to
